mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates directly increase the discount rate applied to equity cash flows, compressing forward S&P 500 valuations and producing negative returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for FEDFUNDS against 20-day forward returns remains below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window, or positive returns occur in >65% of rate increase periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending power, triggering negative equity returns within the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward 20-day returns are positive in >60% of instances where UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp threshold, or the signal loses statistical significance in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and credit availability, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive Rank IC greater than 0.04 over 60-day horizons, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of co-expansion periods.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and corporate earnings momentum, generating positive short-term equity returns during economic expansions.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are negative in >55% of INDPRO acceleration periods, or the mechanism predictive power vanishes during contraction phases with Rank IC below zero.

mechanism_narrative: Core inflation exceeding the 3.0 percent threshold forces expectations of aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: S&P 500 delivers positive 20-day returns in >60% of high-inflation threshold crossings, or the signal shows no predictive edge with Rank IC below 0.02 in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payroll growth combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports corporate revenue growth, yielding positive quarterly equity returns.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal produces a negative Rank IC over 60-day horizons, or positive forward returns occur in less than 45% of confirmed tightening episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real GDP growth directly correlates with improved corporate profit margins and earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for GDPC1 against 20-day forward returns falls below 0.01 over a 3-year rolling window, or returns are negative in >60% of positive GDP growth quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A divergence where industrial production declines while inflation remains elevated signals a stagflationary supply shock, triggering rapid negative equity returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns are positive in >55% of stagflation signal activations, or the interaction term fails to achieve statistical significance in out-of-sample regression.
